Notice Of Motion And Motion To Trifurcate Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS ARGOZ, INC., FIVE MILL, INC., DAN SOHA's Motion To Trifurcate Proceedings is GRANTED as to the request to bifurcate the amount of punitive damages from all other issues and DENIED as to the request for a separate trial phase on alter ego liability without prejudice to re-raising that request before the trial judge. Defendants have a statutory right to bifurcation of the amount of punitive damages and that request is not opposed. The trial judge, not a pre-trial judge, is in the best position to evaluate whether a separate trial phase on alter ego liability would promote judicial economy and the interests of justice.

Counsel for defendants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
J. Erik Heath, Esq. 415-433-0333 x7 of DPLO for Plaintiff Nathan Kohrs.
Oleg Albert, Esq. 415-445-0900 of Le Clerc & Le Clerc for Defendant Argoz Inc., Soha, Five Mill.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAN SOHA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. Plaintiff can assert the cited causes of action against his employer (Argoz, Inc.) The undisputed evidence shows that Mr. Soha was not a party to the employment agreement and he cannot be liable as a corporate agent. The court rejects plaintiff's argument that Mr. Soha is liable to plaintiff as the alter ego of Argoz, Inc. The complaint at paragraphs 44-60 alleges that Five Mill, Inc. is the alter ego of Argoz, Inc.. There are no allegations that Mr. Soha is the alter ego of Argoz, Inc. Plaintiff cannot oppose summary adjudication based on a new theory that is not alleged in the complaint. (See Distefano v. Forester (2001) 85 Cal.App.4th 1249, 1264 ("To create a triable issue of material fact, the opposition evidence must be directed to issues raised by the pleadings.").) Accordingly, the motion is granted in its entirety.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840, mlyon@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents And Things; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Argoz Inc.'s Motion to Compel Documents and Things is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant is entitled to obtain inspection, copying, testing, or sampling of tangible things. C.C.P. Section 2031.10. Although generally the party responding to discovery is responsible for the costs of the response, in certain circumstances the requesting party must pay the costs. Toshiba Am. Electronic Components, Inc., 124 Cal. App. 4th 762, 769 (2004). Here, good cause exists to require production of Plaintiff's laptop computer to a mutually agreeable vendor in response to Defendant's Inspection Demand No. 20. Defendant shall pay the costs of the agreed-upon vendor. The parties shall meet and confer to establish mutually agreeable, reasonable procedures designed to ensure that Defendant or his counsel do not obtain access to Plaintiff's personal information, including information covered by the attorney client privilege, stored on the laptop. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. The moving party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge Pro Tem: Greg Call; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
